content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both genuinely need this file (my feature: opt-in debug=False trace logging at start-detection/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset points). My edits are purely additive: (1) add debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None params to __init__ signature, (2) add self.debug/self._logger in __init__ body, (3) insert guarded 'if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): self._logger.debug(...)' lines at 4 spots inside receive() (the allow_reuse reset block, the start-identifier-found block, the end_identifier regex-match block, the StreamResponse return), (4) one guarded log line in flush() before its returns, (5) a small private truncate helper. I will NOT touch receive()/flush() signatures, control flow, or ordering of existing conditions - only insert log lines and the new __init__ params. Proposing sequencing to avoid clobbering: I'll go first since my diff is small and isolated, write patch.txt, then send DONE - after that you can safely add tick()/idle_timeout_s on top. OK to proceed in that order?
